comment.body: Could someone explain this? Does it mean we start building up hedges via Put ratio spreads or put calendar spreads? not sure the flies though.
comment.raw_body: Could someone explain this? Does it mean we start building up hedges via Put ratio spreads or put calendar spreads? not sure the flies though.

comment.body:
I threw that into Gemini and got this. Also asked it to explain the flies etc. 

"I don’t think the market will fall down really hard—just a little dip. If that happens, it might be a good time to make some smart bets for the year. But until I’m sure the dip is real, I use special tools (like flies, ratios, and calendars) to protect myself in case things get bumpy in the short term"


🎯 Flies (Butterfly Spreads) Imagine you’re playing a game where you bet on how high someone will jump. You think they’ll land somewhere in the middle — not too low, not too high. So you place bets on three spots: low, middle, and high. You win the most if they land in the middle! That’s like a butterfly spread — it works best when the market stays near a certain price.
🪢 Ratios (Ratio Spreads) This is like trading marbles with your friends. You give away more marbles than you get back, hoping the ones you keep become super valuable. In trading, you sell more options than you buy, betting that the market won’t move too wildly — but if it does, you’re ready to catch some action.
📅 Calendar Spreads Think of this like planning two birthday parties — one this month and one next month. You expect the second party to be more exciting, so you bet on that one lasting longer. In trading, you buy one option for later and sell one for sooner, hoping the market gets more exciting over time.

But until I’m sure the dip is real, I use special tools (like flies, ratios, and calendars) to protect myself in case things get bumpy in the short term"


🎯 Flies (Butterfly Spreads) Imagine you’re playing a game where you bet on how high someone will jump. You think they’ll land somewhere in the middle — not too low, not too high. So you place bets on three spots: low, middle, and high. You win the most if they land in the middle! That’s like a butterfly spread — it works best when the market stays near a certain price.
🪢 Ratios (Ratio Spreads) This is like trading marbles with your friends. You give away more marbles than you get back, hoping the ones you keep become super valuable. In trading, you sell more options than you buy, betting that the market won’t move too wildly — but if it does, you’re ready to catch some action.
📅 Calendar Spreads Think of this like planning two birthday parties — one this month and one next month. You expect the second party to be more exciting, so you bet on that one lasting longer. In trading, you buy one option for later and sell one for sooner, hoping the market gets more exciting over time.

comment.body:
Basically Alma called the april dip well and positioned herself well by buying that dip. Most of her portfolio is long so she only needs to hedge against downside using spreads or option writing. 

Someone who was not in this chat and joined late or did not buy the April dip either has to enter late with buy the dip or has to wait for the 5% pullback which has been non existent. Missing the april window has been brutal for many.

comment.body:
I threw that into Gemini and got this. Also asked it to explain the flies etc. 

"I don’t think the market will fall down really hard—just a little dip. If that happens, it might be a good time to make some smart bets for the year. But until I’m sure the dip is real, I use special tools (like flies, ratios, and calendars) to protect myself in case things get bumpy in the short term"


🎯 Flies (Butterfly Spreads) Imagine you’re playing a game where you bet on how high someone will jump. You think they’ll land somewhere in the middle — not too low, not too high. So you place bets on three spots: low, middle, and high. You win the most if they land in the middle! That’s like a butterfly spread — it works best when the market stays near a certain price.
🪢 Ratios (Ratio Spreads) This is like trading marbles with your friends. You give away more marbles than you get back, hoping the ones you keep become super valuable. In trading, you sell more options than you buy, betting that the market won’t move too wildly — but if it does, you’re ready to catch some action.
📅 Calendar Spreads Think of this like planning two birthday parties — one this month and one next month. You expect the second party to be more exciting, so you bet on that one lasting longer. In trading, you buy one option for later and sell one for sooner, hoping the market gets more exciting over time.

comment.body:
this means, i still long on the markets over all, don't forget my educational post about the casino. 
and i still don't expect any crash, only a pullback. 
the option structures are there to hedge front end vol, that i was writing about, and the cost is mitigated. this means, i'm not running to short this market. 
i go net short only if structure breaks. 

the fly is butterfly spread, it can be long iron fly or short fly. this is when you bet on vol up move into any direction. (i prefer iron flies tbh, but depends on the taste)
